channel.title: Storage on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Storage on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks natively storing data in Microsoft OneLake
item.description:
Azure Databricks now supports natively writing managed Delta tables to Microsoft OneLake in public preview. This lets customers use OneLake as the storage layer for Databricks workloads without managing separate storage accounts.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Spotlight on SIG Storage
item.description:
SIG Storage discusses its role in Kubernetes persistent storage, including CSI/COSI, data protection, and emerging work for AI workloads. Recent updates include VolumeGroupSnapshot GA in v1.36, CSI Changed Block Tracking Beta in v1.36, and VolumeAttributesClass GA in v1.34.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure HorizonDB
item.description:
Azure HorizonDB is in public preview as a PostgreSQL-compatible database service. The preview highlights autoscaling storage up to 128 TB and claimed performance improvements over self-managed PostgreSQL.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files cache volumes
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files cache volumes are now generally available. The feature adds cloud-based cache volumes that store only the most actively accessed data from an external origin volume.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Moving Volume Group Snapshots to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 promotes volume group snapshots to GA, with the API graduating to groupsnapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1. The feature lets CSI-backed storage drivers take crash-consistent snapshots across multiple PVCs using label selectors and restore workloads from those grouped snapshots.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: AVS Support for AV64 SKU on Azure Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN datastores are now generally available with AV64 SKUs for Azure VMware Solution. This adds a higher-scale, higher-performance storage option for those deployments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Elastic SAN support for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN support is now generally available for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud. It simplifies datastore connectivity and configuration by removing the need for an ExpressRoute gateway and using a single Private Endpoint, while also improving performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Single Volume Snapshots on Azure Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports single volume snapshots in general availability. The feature provides incremental, point-in-time backups for individual SAN volumes, storing snapshots within the SAN.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Connect to Elastic SAN from Windows VM via VM Extension
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports connecting volumes to Windows virtual machines through the Elastic SAN VM extension in the Azure Portal. This can be done during VM deployment.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: SELinux Volume Label Changes goes GA (and likely implications in v1.37)
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 introduces stable Pod field spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy and a new optional selinux-warning-controller to help prepare for a likely v1.37 default-on SELinuxMount change. When SELinux is enabled, mounts can switch from recursive relabeling to mount-time labeling, which improves performance but can break some shared-volume patterns; clusters should audit for conflicts and opt out per Pod with Recursive if needed.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Premium SSD v2 is now generally available for Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. It is positioned to deliver up to 4x higher IOPS, lower latency, and improved price-performance for I/O-intensive workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: ハル (Haru)
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 (Haru) ships 70 enhancements: 18 stable, 25 beta, and 25 alpha. Highlights include GA for fine-grained kubelet API authorization, user namespaces in pods, external ServiceAccount token signing, SELinux volume label changes, and removal of the gitRepo volume driver; Service.spec.externalIPs is deprecated for removal in v1.43.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Storage Mover now available in Azure Government (US)
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover is now generally available in Azure Government (US). It provides a fully managed way for U.S. government customers and partners to migrate large-scale file data into Azure Government cloud environments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box now supports Azure Files Provisioned v2
item.description:
Azure Data Box now supports ingesting data into Azure Files Provisioned v2 storage accounts. Azure Files Provisioned v2 lets customers provision and pay for specific capacity, IOPS, and throughput.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description:
User delegation SAS is now generally available for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues. It provides a more secure SAS option than account or service SAS by binding the token to the delegator.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in US Gov Arizona
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in US Gov Arizona. The disk type is a next-generation block storage option for Azure VMs and is available in a region without Availability Zones.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in South India
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in South India. The region does not have Availability Zones, and the disk type provides sub-millisecond latency as a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure VMs.

  • Source: Azure Updates
